content: Create a workflow diagram that shows the entire workflow for all commands in GSD-T.
Right — the previous edit was blocked, so the file is unchanged. Let me retry with the live timestamp.

Done. Created [docs/workflow-diagram.md](docs/workflow-diagram.md) with 6 Mermaid diagrams covering the full GSD-T command surface:

1. **Top-level map** — entry-point routing from `/gsd` smart router into the lifecycle
2. **Milestone lifecycle** — the core `milestone → partition → plan → impact → execute → test-sync → integrate → verify → complete-milestone` flow with auto-spawned QA / Red Team / Design Verify gates
3. **Wave mode** — hands-off chaining (`/gsd-t-wave`)
4. **Design-to-code pipeline** — `design-decompose → design-build` two-terminal review loop with `design-review` and `design-audit`
5. **Backlog subsystem** — `backlog-add/list/edit/move/remove/promote` feeding into milestone/quick/debug/feature
6. **Automation, observability & utilities** — unattended supervision, visualize/metrics/status/health, session control, maintenance, headless/CI

Plus a phase-successor table and an auto-spawned-subagent table at the bottom. Source of truth is `commands/gsd-t-help.md` Command Summaries — diagram references will track that file as commands change.

Decision Log entry added to `.gsd-t/progress.md`.
Any suggestion to make the diagrams a little more attractive and polished looking?

All 6 diagrams render correctly. Done.

**Rendered files:**
- [01-top-level-map.png](docs/diagrams/01-top-level-map.png) — entry-point routing
- [02-milestone-lifecycle.png](docs/diagrams/02-milestone-lifecycle.png) — core workflow
- [03-wave-mode.png](docs/diagrams/03-wave-mode.png) — hands-off chain
- [04-design-to-code.png](docs/diagrams/04-design-to-code.png) — Figma → contracts → build
- [05-backlog.png](docs/diagrams/05-backlog.png) — backlog subsystem
- [06-automation-utilities.png](docs/diagrams/06-automation-utilities.png) — supervision/observability/maintenance

[docs/workflow-diagram.md](docs/workflow-diagram.md) now embeds each PNG above its Mermaid source — renders in any markdown viewer, plain GitHub view included. Source `.mmd` files kept alongside in [docs/diagrams/](docs/diagrams/) so the diagrams can be re-rendered if commands change (`npx -p @mermaid-js/mermaid-cli mmdc -i NN-name.mmd -o NN-name.png -b white -w 1600 -s 2`).

Fri: May 8, 2026 09:29 PDT — GSD-T v3.24.10 — CURRENT

→ Conversational mode (no command spawn)

A few high-leverage upgrades, in order of effort-vs-impact:

**Quick wins (5 min, huge polish bump):**
1. **Switch theme to `neutral` or `base` with a custom `themeVariables` block.** The default Mermaid palette is washed-out. A custom theme with a tighter color story (one accent, two supporting tints, one neutral) will make all 6 diagrams feel like a set instead of six separately-colored Excel charts.
2. **Render to SVG instead of PNG.** Sharper at any zoom, smaller file, and the text stays selectable/searchable. Same `mmdc` command, just `-o foo.svg`.
3. **Bump font to something more deliberate.** Add `themeVariables: { fontFamily: 'Inter, ui-sans-serif, system-ui' }` (or whatever Tekyz uses) — the default Trebuchet MS dates the diagrams immediately.

**Medium effort, big impact:**
4. **Reduce node count per diagram.** Diagram 1 (top-level map) has ~20 nodes — at that density it stops being a diagram and becomes a graph. Group `init/scan/setup` into a single "Onboarding" subgraph node, same for all the design commands. Aim for ~8–12 nodes per top-level diagram.
5. **Consistent visual language across all 6.** Pick one shape per role and apply it everywhere:
   - `[ ]` for commands
   - `( )` for user/output endpoints
   - `{ }` for decisions
   - `[[ ]]` or subgraphs for grouped concepts
   - One color per role (command vs subagent vs gate vs artifact) — same colors in every diagram.
6. **Use `LR` orientation for the milestone lifecycle.** Pipelines read better left-to-right; top-down works for trees. The wave diagram already does this — the milestone lifecycle would too.

**Higher effort, optional:**
7. **Replace text in nodes with icons + short labels.** "🎯 milestone", "🔨 execute", "✅ verify" — Mermaid supports unicode in node text. Cuts visual weight.
8. **Add a single "legend" diagram at the top** showing what each shape/color means, so the rest stays clean.
9. **Consider Excalidraw or D2 for hero diagrams.** Mermaid is great for flowcharts; if you want the top-level map to feel like a polished marketing diagram, D2 (`d2 lang`) renders much more attractively out of the box. Keep Mermaid for the procedural ones.

**My recommendation:** start with #1 + #2 + #4 + #5 — that's ~30 min of work and will get you 80% of the polish. Want me to do that pass?
Yes, the redraw the diagrams with D2 and implement suggestions 3 & 5
Now update the legend at the top to match the new D2 visual language, add a note about how to regenerate, and append the Decision Log entry.
